title: menarda nummulariifolia
intro: Menarda nummulariifolia, also known as mint geranium or mint leaf geranium, is a perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the mountains of Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Menarda nummulariifolia include reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and promoting digestion. Menarda nummulariifolia is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing with a solvent like ethanol.
